Name: Schedule regular supervisor meetings
Notes: Set up recurring meetings with your thesis supervisor (recommended: bi-weekly). Add meeting notes and action items as subtasks.

Name: Create detailed thesis timeline
Notes: Break down the 4-6 month project into phases with specific milestones. Include buffer time for revisions and unexpected delays.

Name: Define thesis topic and research questions
Notes: Finalize your thesis topic, research questions, and objectives with your supervisor. Document the scope and boundaries of your research.

Name: Finalize research methodology
Notes: Define and document your research methods, data collection procedures, and analysis approach. Get supervisor approval.

Name: Complete literature review
Notes: Conduct comprehensive literature review. Organize sources and identify gaps in existing research that your thesis will address.

Name: Incorporate supervisor feedback
Notes: Review and implement feedback from supervisor on draft chapters. Track changes and maintain version control.

Name: Complete first draft of all chapters
Notes: Write first drafts of introduction, methodology, results, discussion, and conclusion. Focus on getting ideas down before perfecting.

Name: Final thesis submission
Notes: Submit final formatted thesis according to institutional guidelines. Verify all requirements: formatting, binding, digital submission, etc.

Name: Ethics approval deadline (if applicable)
Notes: Submit ethics application if your research involves human subjects or sensitive data. Allow 4-6 weeks for approval process.

Name: Submit thesis proposal (if required)
Notes: Prepare and submit formal thesis proposal including research questions, methodology, and timeline. Check institutional requirements.

Name: Prepare for thesis defense (if required)
Notes: Create presentation, anticipate questions, and practice your defense. Schedule mock defense with peers or supervisor.

Name: Proofread and format thesis
Notes: Complete thorough proofreading for grammar, spelling, and formatting. Ensure compliance with institutional style guide and formatting requirements.

Name: Organize reference library
Notes: Set up reference management system (Zotero, Mendeley, EndNote). Maintain organized library of all sources throughout the project.
